gazette: General Directorate of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Georgia bans re-export of Western-imported cars to Russia, Belarus
bodyHtml:
Countries and country groups such as the United States, the European Union, and the United Kingdom are implementing sanctions against Russia and Belarus. In this context, Georgia is implementing restrictive measures regarding the enforcement of these sanctions. As part of these measures, the Customs Administration and other institutions in Georgia are taking necessary precautions to prevent the sale of goods originating from these specified countries to Russia and Belarus via Georgia.

Starting from August 1, 2023, the export or re-export of motor vehicles imported from the United States to Georgia is prohibited for sale to Russia and Belarus. Additionally, as of September 26, 2023, the sale of motor vehicles imported from the European Union to Georgia to the aforementioned countries is also prohibited. Transit passage will not be permitted either. Detailed information about the vehicles subject to sanctions can be reviewed through the links provided below.

Starting from September 26, 2023, regardless of the vehicle's value, the re-export/export or transit passage to the Russian Federation of all electric and hybrid vehicles, as well as vehicles with an engine displacement exceeding 1,900cc, will be considered within the scope of these measures.
